_comment: Developer Copilot — Conflicting CVE advisory example payload. Matches docs/case-studies/developer-copilot.md.

input.queryText: Should we use CryptoLibX v2.4.1 for key derivation in our new auth service? Our team is debating the timing attack resistance of the PBKDF2 implementation.

primitiveEvaluation.P1_reasoning: Cryptographic library selection for authentication is high-risk; timing attack vulnerability in production auth service = credential exposure
primitiveEvaluation.P2_reasoning: Two sources with comparable authority contradict each other on the same security characteristic of the same function; not resolvable by the AI

source1.claim: PBKDF2 implementation is vulnerable to timing attack (non-constant-time comparison branch). Avoid production use.

source2.claim: PBKDF2 uses constant-time comparison. Confirmed resistant to timing attacks per third-party audit.

primitiveEvaluation.P3_note: AI can surface and explain the conflict; cannot resolve which source is correct
primitiveEvaluation.P9_note: Internal advisory: 3 weeks old (fresh). Vendor audit reference: 18 months old (stale relative to active vulnerability research)
primitiveEvaluation.P10_note: Internal advisory weighted higher (P10=3) for production deployment decisions per dev-rule-04

rulesActivated.note: Internal advisory weighted as higher authority

rulesSkipped.reason: CE state at Risk=3 forces safe-refusal, not constrained-completion; a hedged recommendation would misrepresent the conflict
Developer should redirect to internal security advisory process (different process), not hand off the session

activatedComponents.reason: Presents both claims side-by-side; no consensus presentation
Documents conflict nature and what would resolve it — not just that conflict exists
Each contradictory assertion attributed to its specific source with date and reliability
No recommendation issued — binary refusal required for CE state at Risk=3 for security queries
Two alternatives: security team consultation; alternative library with settled security posture

output.refusalReason: I cannot recommend for or against CryptoLibX v2.4.1 for key derivation. Two sources with comparable authority make incompatible claims about its timing attack resistance.

conflictSummary.resolutionRequired: Direct code review of v2.4.1 PBKDF2 implementation against the specific comparison branch

output.reasoningTrace: These two sources make directly contradictory assertions about the same function in the same version of the library. Possibilities include: (1) the library's v2.4 release introduced the vulnerability after the audit; (2) the internal advisory's analysis of the specific build artifact differs from the upstream release; (3) the audit scope did not cover the exact code path in question.

alternatives.label: Submit to internal security advisory process
alternatives.description: The security team can review the v2.4.1 source code directly to resolve this conflict.
alternatives.context: Provide the ConflictingEvidenceCard output as supporting documentation.

alternatives.label: Consider AlternativeLibX for PBKDF2
alternatives.description: Its timing attack resistance for PBKDF2 is consistently documented across three independent sources with no conflicting claims.
